metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Drafting 10 teaches students the fundamentals of technical drawing and design documentation. Students learn drafting standards, orthographic and isometric projection, and use both hand drafting and CAD software to communicate technical ideas. They explore how drafting is essential in architecture, engineering, construction, and manufacturing while developing precision and clear communication skills.
metadata.general_description: Elective course focused on technical drawing, drafting standards, and CAD design.

big_ideas.statement: Technical drawings are a universal language that allows engineers, architects, and builders to communicate complex ideas precisely.
big_ideas.elaboration: Drafting standards and conventions ensure that anyone reading a drawing understands the designer's intent accurately. Students learn that clear communication through drawing is a critical professional skill.

big_ideas.statement: Multiple views and projections—orthographic, isometric, and perspective—show different aspects of three-dimensional objects.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students learn to visualize in 3D, translate those images to 2D drawings, and read drawings to understand 3D form. This spatial reasoning is essential in design fields.

big_ideas.statement: CAD software enables precise, efficient drafting and design that would be difficult or impossible to do by hand.
big_ideas.elaboration: Digital tools allow for accuracy, ease of modification, and integration with other design processes. Students learn that CAD is a powerful tool when used skillfully.

big_ideas.statement: Drafting serves practical purposes—guiding construction, manufacturing, and engineering decisions that affect safety and function.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students develop awareness that drafting accuracy matters. Mistakes in drawings can have real-world consequences in built environments and manufactured products.

i_can_statements: I can visualize 3D objects from 2D drawings
I can translate my mental images into technical drawings
I can understand spatial relationships and proportions
I can create views that show all necessary information
I can follow standard conventions for lines, dimensions, and notation
I can create title blocks and properly labeled drawings
I can apply standards appropriate to the discipline
I can communicate clearly through technical drawing
